lede: A look at January 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including J. J. McCarthy and Kevin Parker, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2018 Inter-Continental Hotel Kabul attack
events.desc: A group of four or five gunmen attack The Inter-Continental Hotel in Kabul, Afghanistan, sparking a 12-hour battle. The attack kills 40 people and injures many others.

events.title: Black January
events.desc: Protests in Azerbaijan, part of the Dissolution of the Soviet Union.

events.title: Pakistan and weapons of mass destruction
events.desc: Pakistan launches its nuclear weapons program, a few weeks after its defeat in the Bangladesh Liberation War, as well as the Indo-Pakistani War of 1971.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: At the Wannsee Conference held in the Berlin suburb of Wannsee, senior Nazi German officials discuss the implementation of the "Final Solution to the Jewish question".

events.title: Turkish Constitution of 1921
events.desc: The first Constitution of Turkey is adopted, making fundamental changes in the source and exercise of sovereignty by consecrating the principle of national sovereignty.

events.title: Battle of Yungay
events.desc: In the Battle of Yungay, Chile defeats an alliance between Peru and Bolivia.

events.title: Johann Sebastian Bach
events.desc: J. S. Bach leads the first performance of his cantata Meine Seufzer, meine Tränen (My sighs, my tears), BWV 13, for the second Sunday after Epiphany.

events.title: Battle of Rio de Janeiro (1567)
events.desc: Battle of Rio de Janeiro: Portuguese forces under the command of Estácio de Sá definitively drive the French out of Rio de Janeiro.

events.title: Edward Balliol
events.desc: Edward Balliol surrenders his claim to the Scottish throne to Edward III in exchange for an English pension.

events.title: Lalli
events.desc: Finnish peasant Lalli kills English clergyman Henry, the Bishop of Turku, on the ice of Lake Köyliö.

births.name: Kevin Parker
births.line: Australian singer, songwriter, musician, and producer

births.name: Stephen Crabb
births.line: Scottish-Welsh politician, Secretary of State for Wales

deaths.text: Audrey Hepburn, British actress and humanitarian activist
Basil Moreau, French priest, founded the Congregation of Holy Cross
